channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Morbid Metal launches in Early Access on April 8
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Shapeshift, chain combos, and tear through waves of enemies.

Roguelike hack-and-slash action game Morbid Metal will launch in Early Access for PC via Steam on April 8 for a limited-time launch price of $13.49 / €13.49, publisher Ubisoft and developer SCREEN JUICE announced. After that, it will cost $17.99 / €17.99.

Here is an overview of the Early Access release:

Early Access Content Summary

Shapeshift into powerful characters in real time, chain stylish combos, and tear through waves of deadly machines and brutal bosses in this fast-paced hack-and-slash roguelite.

The full Early Access Roadmap will be revealed on April 17, during Find Your Next Game Showcase.

Key Features

  • Three playable characters
  • First bosses
  • 10 enemy types
  • Three biomes / unique runs with mid-run modifiers
    • Mid-Run Modifiers
      • Over 60 routines (mid-run perks)
      • Protocols (character attacks)
      • Corpora (mid-run effects)
    • Mid-Run Interactables
      • Devil Bargain (risk-reward)
      • Eden’s Blessing (routine upgrade station)
      • And the list goes on!
    • Optional Encounters
      • Emporium (mid-run shop)
      • Operators Trial (optional challenges)
  • Advanced Meta / Progression
    • Unlock the Protocols – Protocols are character-specific and significantly alter combat flow, allowing you to adapt and refine your playstyle. (Unlocked protocols can appear during runs and be selected as rewards.)
    • Work on Synergies – unlock or upgrade specific effects and enable synergies across your loadout during the run. (Corpora slots can be unlocked through the neural nexus.)
    • Upgrade Void Nexus – A skill-tree system offering permanent, incremental upgrades.
    • Explore Lore Codex Entries

Languages

  • English
  • German
  • Spanish
  • Japanese
  • Simplified Chinese
  • Brazilian Portuguese

During Early Access, we will be considering adding other languages, so don’t worry if your preferred language isn’t there yet.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Early Access Release Date Trailer

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/morbid-metal-launches-in-early-access-on-april-8
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Starward coming to PS5 on April 16
item.content:encoded:
• PS5

Wishlisting now available.

Publisher Century Games and developer Game Blender Studio will release 3D fighting game Starward for PlayStation 5 on April 16, the companies announced. Wishlisting is now available via PlayStation Store.

Starward is currently available for PC via Steam, iOS via App Store, and Android via Google Play.

Here is an overview of the game, via Century Games:

About

Starward is a 3D fighting game that integrates mecha girls and classic Gundam vs. Gundam combat gameplay, with 1V1 and 2V2 mode as the core gameplay. In Starward, players can control mecha girls with distinct personalities and different combat styles, and experience three-dimensional combat experiences beyond the ground.

Classic Gundam vs. Gundam-like Gameplay

Starward faithfully recreates the Gundam vs. Gundam gameplay that has been popular in Japanese arcade halls for over a decade. It includes actions such as Beam Rifle, Machine Gun, Charge Shoot, fighting, blocking, stepping, dashing, allowing you to quickly showcase your skill level.

The Starward experience spans across three distinct game modes featuring two-versus-two, one-versus-one, and arcade gameplay. Player-versus-player is the main, featuring fast-paced battles lasting about three minutes per match.

Mecha Girls VERSUS

All characters are mecha girls, skilled in close-range fighting or long-range shooting. You can choose characters like Aliz, SkySaber, XiaoLing to play as vanguards; or characters like Elfin, Cavalry, Hikari to play as rearguards.

Each character has its unique design, such as Chinese style, elves, witches, fully armed mecha girls, and more.

The COST System

The character’s combat power “COST” is divided into four categories: 3.0 / 2.5 / 2.0 / 1.5. You need to allocate the “COST” of the team to achieve the most reasonable configuration.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/starward-coming-to-ps5-on-april-16
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: State of Decay 3 alpha tests to begin in May
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Xbox Series

Registration now available.

Publisher Xbox Game Studios and developer Undead Labs will begin hosting a series of alpha tests for the 2020-announced State of Decay 3, which we last saw in 2024, starting in May, the companies announced. Registration is available now via the official website.

The alpha tests will feature four-player cooperative play, new base building and resource strategies, and “a whole lot of combat,” according to Undead Labs’ Brant Fitzgerald.

“If scavenging supplies in the middle of a zombie outbreak sounds fun to you, then grab your ruck, pack some mags, and head over to our website to find more information and register for a chance to be included in the alpha,” Fitzgerald said. “If you don’t get contacted for this round, don’t worry, we’ll keep your name on the list and have plenty of other opportunities for you throughout the year.”

State of Decay 3 is in development for Xbox Series and PC (Steam, Microsoft Store).

Watch the alpha announcement video below.

Alpha Announce Video



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/state-of-decay-3-alpha-tests-to-begin-in-may
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Thick As Thieves now a single-player and co-op-focused game
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

New gameplay trailer set for Triple-i Initiative on April 9.

Thick As Thieves, the Megabit-published multiplayer stealth game from Warren Spector first announced in December 2024, is now a single-player and two-player cooperative play-focused game, developer OtherSide Entertainment said in an update.

After nearly a year-and-a-half of silence, a new gameplay trailer will debut at Triple-i Initiative on April 9.

Here is the full message from OtherSide Entertainment:

Fellow Thieves,

We’re just under two weeks away from the Triple-I Initiative on [April 9]! At the show, we’ll be lifting the veil on Thick as Thieves‘ brand-new gameplay trailer alongside some other well-guarded secrets. But before all that, we want to share an update.

When we first revealed Thick as Thieves we intended to focus on [player-versus-player-versus-environment] gameplay. However, as development progressed and the world of Kilcairn has come to life, we found that we were having more fun with solo and [cooperative] play.

So, we’ve taken a decision we feel is right for the game: Thick as Thieves is now focused on two-player [cooperative play] and single-player.

This sharpened focus has allowed us to double down on what makes Thick as Thieves truly special—dynamic stealth gameplay. We’re already seeing how this has strengthened the moment-to-moment action, whether you’re playing solo or with a partner in [cooperative play]. We’ve had some fantastic moments while testing the game. Now we’re putting all our energy into making things even better as we build up to launch.

We’re so excited show you more of Thick as Thieves at the Triple-i Initiative. In the meantime, come and chat all things Thick as Thieves with us in our official Discord server.

Your partners in crime,
OtherSide Entertainment

Thick As Thieves is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store in 2026.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/thick-as-thieves-now-a-single-player-and-co-op-focused-game
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
